purpose: CPU-vs-GPU reference-equivalence leg. The sibling fixture nomsa_200step_1sample_3recycle_bf16 (no _gpu suffix) is the SAME official Boltz-2 run on CPU; this one is the SAME run on an NVIDIA GPU. The two are compared to show the reference is hardware-invariant (CPU ~= GPU), so a pharma evaluator running on GPU can accept the CPU-generated reference fixtures as representative.
reference_commit: boltz 2.2.1 (pip, upstream jwohlwend/boltz) — identical to the CPU fixture
command: boltz predict trpcage_no_msa.yaml --out_dir <out> --seed <N> --recycling_steps 3 --sampling_steps 200 --diffusion_samples 1 --accelerator gpu  (then scripts/boltz2_ref_layout.py to reshape to structures/<tid>.cif + results.json)
provenance: Generated 2026-07-13 on a rented vast.ai on-demand RTX 3090 (instance 44721818). Same official Boltz-2 2.2.1, same target, same settings, same seeds (0, 1) as the committed CPU fixture nomsa_200step_1sample_3recycle_bf16. Each seed's GPU predict ran in ~10s after the model weights were cached. Comparison output: docs/pharma-benchmark-data/boltz2-cpu-vs-gpu-ref.json.

result.metric: Kabsch CA-RMSD, GPU-vs-CPU (X) vs CPU-vs-CPU floor (R)
result.verdict: GPU reference agrees with CPU reference to within the CPU reference's own run-to-run noise floor (X=0.68 A <= R=0.81 A). Confidence deltas (GPU-CPU): confidence_score +0.0152, ptm +0.0591, complex_plddt +0.0042, complex_pde -0.0166 — all within 0.06. The reference is hardware-invariant.

invalidation_rule: Regenerate this fixture ONLY when the pinned reference_version/commit changes or the settings above change. For any other change the fixture is reused as-is.
